text: The surgeon gains safe access to the abdomen using a visualized trocar port, revealing the peritoneum upon entry. During diagnostic laparoscopy, a single adhesion band from the transverse colon to the mesentery is identified as the cause of proximal ileum obstruction. Sharp dissection with scissors is performed to lyse the adhesion, with caution advised regarding heat effects on the bowel.
The surgeon inspects the entire intestine, visually examining from the duodenal junction to the ileocecal valve, ensuring no remaining obstructions or abnormalities.
